exemplar.title: Modelling the face of the Great Sphinx and determining its volume

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling the face of the Great Sphinx and determining its volume

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Comprehensive and well-formatted table of contents enhancing navigation
Clear macro-structure with numbered headings and subheadings
Consistent captioning of figures and referencing of tables
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and repeated definitions interrupt logical flow
Abrupt transitions between sections (e.g., facial features)
Excessive descriptive detail and back-tracking reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is well organized with a clear structure (band 3) and shows some overall coherence, but extended tangential prose, redundant definitions and occasional narrative back-tracking prevent it reaching the highest band. A score of 3 reflects its solid macro-organization and logical layout, tempered by lapses in conciseness and smooth development.

strengths: Effective use of algebraic, graphical (Desmos screenshots) and tabular representations
Derivation of the piecewise linear formula is accurate and clearly communicated
Colour-coded graphs and multiple representations reinforce the mathematical argument
weaknesses: Frequent notation inaccuracies (e.g., misprints âLagrandeâ, inconsistent subscripts)
Many equations lack explicit units and contextual definitions
Low resolution of some images diminishes clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are sporadic notation errors and image-quality issues, the student consistently employs appropriate mathematical language and delivers multiple, effective representations with clear logical progression. This performance aligns with band 4 for Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Choice of the Sphinx and point-placement strategy shows significant independent thinking
Colour-coding of function families demonstrates a clear personal approach
Use of mixed interpolation methods indicates good creativity and initiative
weaknesses: Personal rationale lacks direct linkage to specific mathematical challenges
Much of the methodology follows standard textbook procedures
Aim statement could be more precise regarding desired accuracy or level of detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs selection of modelling strategies and personal touches provide clear evidence of independent thinking and creativity (band 2), but the approach remains largely conventional and not sufficiently innovative to merit the highest band.

strengths: Acknowledges limitations in symmetry and pixelation issues
Identifies technology dependency and some model weaknesses
weaknesses: Reflection is largely descriptive with no quantitative error analysis
No deep critical analysis or exploration of alternative methods
Outcome evaluation lacks future implications or improvement plan
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but remains superficial and sporadic, offering basic comments without probing analysis or comprehensive evaluation of results (band 1). The limited depth and absence of critical, forward-looking reflection justify a score of 1.

strengths: Diverse and relevant use of functions (polynomial, exponential, trigonometric) throughout
Correct formulation of the piecewise volume integral and informed handling of vertical segments
Mathematics applied is fully commensurate with SL expectations and well-integrated into the model
weaknesses: Occasional algebraic slips and derivation gaps (e.g., failed Excel method not fully derived)
Insufficient justification for choice of specific interpolation methods
Minor notation and substitution errors in radical function construction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs a wide range of course-level mathematics effectively and shows thorough understanding, with only minor errors and some missing justifications. This aligns with band 5 for Use of Mathematics (SL), reflecting comprehensive and well-applied mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations of why particular functions were chosen or how parameters were obtained are present but often convoluted or partially correct.  Logical links inside derivations are sometimes missing, so clarity is inconsistent, corresponding to âsome logical structure and clarityâ.

The student usually manipulates the mathematics correctly, yet several derivations contain algebraic slips (e.g., undefined slope, mis-placed roots) and notation errors.  Conceptual understanding of symmetry and parametric rearrangement is partial.  This aligns with âgood understanding with minor errorsâ, i.e., band 3.

The student provides equations, Desmos screenshots, colour-coded graphs and extensive point tables, offering several representations of the model.  Although the quality of some images is low, the use of algebraic, graphical and tabular forms is âeffectiveâ overall.

The student follows a recognisable path: rationale â methodology â construction â volume â conclusion.  However, extensive digressions (e.g., repeated definitions, long quotations of formulas, and detailed point tables) interrupt the logical flow.  Links between successive sections (ears, eyes, mouth, jaw) are sometimes abrupt and the narrative occasionally back-tracks to correct earlier work.  Overall, the exploration âshows some coherenceâ, but not the smoother, well-sequenced argument expected for a higher band.

Using 107 mixed function types, piecewise smoothing and reflection demonstrates creativity and initiative beyond a routine treatment.  Yet most techniques are standard and heavily supported by technology, so the work fits the middle band rather than âexceptionalâ.

The student notes model limitations (asymmetry, smoothing issues) but analysis is descriptive rather than probing; there is no investigation of error size or alternative methods.  This corresponds to âbasic analysis of resultsâ.

Outcome evaluation is limited to stating that the volume lacks meaning because the face is not symmetrical; no quantitative accuracy check or future improvement plan is offered.  Hence only âlimited evaluation of outcomesâ is demonstrated.

Reflection is confined to a brief âConclusionâ and âEvaluationâ discussing difficulties, technology use, and picture resolution.  These comments are surface-level and sporadic, providing only âlimited reflectionâ.

Standard notation is attempted, but there are frequent inaccuracies (âLagrandeâ, misplaced subscripts, inconsistent rounding).  Units and parameters are sometimes omitted, yet core symbols (â, Ï, ln, domains) are used.  This intermittent correctness aligns with âsome appropriate mathematical language and notationâ.

The studentâs modelling choices (e.g., compromise between detail and manageability, colour-coding of function families) show a clear personal stamp, satisfying âclear evidence of personal approachâ but not rising to an innovative level.

The text is extremely long and often repeats definitions (e.g., interpolation types) and point lists.  Much descriptive prose is only tangentially relevant to the mathematics.  While the core material is present, conciseness is lacking and some irrelevant detail remains, so the strand fits âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ.

A wide range of functions, parametric rearrangements and a definite integral for volume are directly tied to modelling the Sphinx, showing mathematics that is clearly relevant and applied rather than decorative.  Usage is appropriate but not yet comprehensive/expert, fitting band 4.

Selecting the Sphinx, determining point placement, and experimenting with several interpolation strategies illustrate âsignificant evidence of independent thinkingâ.  However, many steps follow textbook procedures without deeper adaptation, preventing placement in the highest band.

A full table of contents, numbered headings and sub-headings, and consistent sectioning (1â12) give the work a clear macro-structure.  Figures are captioned and most tables are referenced.  Despite some misplaced images and minor formatting glitches, the reader can locate material with ease, so the work is âwell organised with clear structureâ.

Polynomial fitting, trigonometric interpolation, radicals, inverse trig and a volume integral are all at or above SL level and fully appropriate for HL expectations.  The breadth and contextual use justify âmathematics fully commensurate with course levelâ (band 4).

annotations.comment: The bibliography is comprehensive and consistently formatted, demonstrating excellent academic referencing practice.
The plotted points figure effectively supports the text. However, improving image resolution and adding axis labels would enhance clarity.
The interpolation introduction succinctly defines the concept, but the step to naming specific methods could be organized into a concise list instead of a long paragraph.

The research question identifies an intriguing modelling goal but remains overly broad. It would benefit from explicitly stating which functions will be compared and the criteria for volume approximation.
The large introductory image is visually striking but distracts from the research question text. Consider resizing or repositioning to maintain reader focus.
The detailed table of contents with clear numbering enhances navigation and demonstrates effective macro-organization.
The placement and styling of section headers in the table of contents could be more concise. Removing redundant entries (e.g., repeated page numbers) would improve conciseness.
The personal rationale conveys genuine engagement through heritage connection but lacks link to specific mathematical challenges to be explored.
The aim statement is clear in intent but could be more precise regarding the target accuracy or level of detail expected in the 2D reconstruction.
The methodology outlines interpolation techniques but does not justify the choice of each type for specific facial features. Add rationale for method selection.
The positioning discussion demonstrates thoughtful consideration of the coordinate system but the technical definition of a quadrant is unnecessary here and interrupts the flow.
The choice to switch from exponential to quadratic modelling for points d, e, f illustrates independent thinking in method adaptation.
The parametric reflection discussion demonstrates awareness of integration prerequisites, yet the handling of domain reversal is convoluted. Write a brief stepâbyâstep diagram.
The volume integral formula $$V = \sum \pi\left|\int_c^d x^2\,dy\right|$$ is correctly stated. Consider explaining the choice of summation in piecewise regions.
The conclusion reiterates achievements but offers only superficial reflection; add quantitative error estimation between model and actual Sphinx dimensions.
The evaluation notes technology dependency and pixelation issues but lacks exploration of alternative manual methodsâconsider discussing potential analytical strategies.
Many equations lack explicit units and context when introduced. Ensure every formula is accompanied by unit definitions for clear mathematical communication.
The derivation of the piecewise linear formula is mathematically accurate, and notation is mostly consistent, supporting clear communication.
The section on Lagrange interpolation contains misprints (âLagrandeâ) and inconsistent variable naming. Ensure correct spelling and unify subscripts for clarity.
The exploration of exponential interpolation techniques shows initiative, but the failed Excel method is described in insufficient mathematical depth â include derivation.
The identification of the undefined slope for a vertical segment demonstrates good problem-solving. The explanation linking this to a vertical line is clear and correct.
The radical function construction via simultaneous equations is logically sound but could be improved by explicitly presenting the system of equations before substitution.
The resolution of the undefined slope by domain restriction shows good critical thinking. Including a graphical inset of the vertical segment would reinforce understanding.
The use of diverse function types (polynomial, exponential, trigonometric) shows strong application of course-level mathematics throughout.
The appendix tables provide exhaustive data but suffer from overcrowding. Consider splitting very large tables into smaller, thematically grouped tables to aid readability.
The narrative occasionally back-tracks to correct earlier steps, which can disrupt the logical flow. Adding brief transitional sentences would improve coherence.
